description: You are helping a technical-writer tidy up a small documentation workspace that lives in /home/user/docs. Three Markdown source files already exist in that directory: /home/user/docs/intro.md /home/user/docs/usage.md /home/user/docs/faq.md Each file’s very first line is a level-1 heading (it begins with “# ”). Your job is to generate two new artefacts inside the **same** directory. 1. /home/user/docs/INDEX.md • The file must start with an H1 heading that literally reads “# Documentation Index” (no extra spaces, exactly that spelling and capitalisation). • Under that heading create a bullet list (one dash “-” followed by a single space) that enumerates **every** Markdown file found directly in /home/user/docs. • For each list item: - Extract the text that appears after “# ” on the first line of the file (this is its title). - Write the item in the exact format: - - <filename> where <filename> is just the base name such as “intro.md” (do **not** include any directory path). • Sort the list items **alphabetically by the title text**. • Ensure the file ends with a single newline. 2. /home/user/docs/index_build.log • A plain-text log that records, one per line, the files you processed and the outcome. • The log must contain exactly the following four lines, in this order: Processed: faq.md Processed: intro.md Processed: usage.md Index generated: INDEX.md (Make sure the filenames match the case of the actual files.) • Finish the log with a trailing newline as well. The verification harness will check that: • /home/user/docs/INDEX.md exists. • Its first line is “# Documentation Index”. • It contains exactly three bullet lines that match the required “- <Title> - <filename>” pattern. • The list is in the correct alphabetical order (FAQ → Introduction → Usage Guide). • /home/user/docs/index_build.log exists and its four lines are exactly as specified above. You do not need to move, rename, or edit the original *.md files—just create the new index and log files. A handful of shell utilities (e.g., <code>grep</code>, <code>awk</code>, <code>sort</code>, and redirection) should be enough; aim to keep the solution to roughly three or four commands.
